mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 4.5% threshold, invalidating the earnings deterioration channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60d returns in >50% of cases, breaking the synchronized demand channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low policy rates reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during easing cycles correlate with negative 5d returns in >60% of observations, indicating supply expansion fails to translate to equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly forecasts corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 60d returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across all tested windows, refuting the revenue expansion link.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 305.0, accelerating inflation forces tighter financial conditions and erodes real earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 305.0 are followed by positive 20d returns in >50% of cases, disproving the inflation-driven earnings erosion mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 20d returns in >55% of triggered months, invalidating the labor tightening premium.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during periods of elevated policy rates indicate demand destruction from financial tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during high-rate regimes fail to predict negative 5d returns (accuracy < 45%), breaking the demand destruction transmission channel.
